instructions_en: 1) Grab a bench or step and lay down on your back (face up). Have your torso flat on the bench with your arms by your sides. One of your feet should be planted on the floor with your knees bent at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees), the other should be extended towards the ceiling as vertical as possible. This is your starting position.

2) To initiate the movement, exhale and drive through your heel to lift your hips toward the ceiling by squeezing your glutes. As you do so, lean back onto your shoulders and keep driving upward until your glutes are fully contracted.

3) After a brief pause, inhale and gently relax your glutes to drop your pelvis towards the floor (the resistance should be placed through your heel). Continue to drop your pelvis until your glutes touch the floor.
4) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name_en: Lateral Squat to Cross Body Row
instructions_en: 1) Grab a resistance band.
2) Have a wide leg stance.
3) Place one end of the band under one foot.
4) With the other hand grab the other end of the band.
5) Transfer weight over towards the foot with the band under and reach the arm towards the foot then come back up to standing and pull the band into a high row position with elbow leading the move.
6) Repeat as many repetitions as prescribed.
1) Place a small band around the thighs just above the knee.
2) Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

3) Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

4) After a brief pause, exhale and return to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
5) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
1) Grab a small band and place it round the thighs just above the knee.

2) Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance with your toes pointing slightly outward. Allow your arms to hang comfortably by your sides at this point. Throughout the movement you should keep your head up and maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position.

3) Inhale and descend slowly by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel with the floor. Keep a straight back so that your posture is not compromised. Bring your arms out in front of you so that they are also parallel to the floor at this point. Tip: When at the bottom of the movement your knees should be behind your toes.

4) Once you have hit the bottom of the movement, exhale and push hard through your heels to drive your hips forward. As you extend at your knee and ankle joints in order to jump as high as you can, pull your arms down towards the sides of your torso at the top of the jump (this will help to generate more height), before landing into your next rep.
5) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
1) Grab a band and place it round the bottom of the feet.
2) Sitting on the floor holding onto the other end of the band, lift the feet off the ground and lean back slightly keeping the head and chest lifted, back straight and abs engaged.
3) Slowly rotate to each side drawing the band towards the hips. After each rep return to the middle and then to the other side.
4) Repeat for the prescribed repetitions.
1) Grab a small band and place it round the thighs just above the knees whilst in a standing position. This will be your starting position.
2) Take a step backwards (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Whilst maintaining a straight torso.
3) Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.
4) As you exhale, use mainly the heel of your front foot and push off the floor towards the starting position again.
5) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and then change sides.
1) Grab a small band and place it round the thighs just above the knees.

2) Starting with feet hip distance apart brace the abs and step out laterally dropping into a squat position by bending the knees and hinging at the hips. Keep the chest lifted and sit back into the heels whilst pushing your knees into the band.
3) Step back to the starting position by pushing out of the ground and then repeat on the other side.
4) Repeat for prescribed repetitions.
1) Grab a small band and place it round the ankles.
2) Take a small step back.
3) Raise the leg squeezing the glutes as you lift and slightly lean forwards.
4) Keep abs engaged and chest lifted throughout.
5) Return the leg back to the start position and then repeat for the prescribed repetitions.
6) Do the same on the other leg.
1) Grab a small band and place it around the thighs just above the knees.
2) Lay on your back with knees bent and feet hip distance apart, knees directly over ankles.
3) Pushing knees into the band and keeping abs braced squeeze your glutes and drive hips up until you have full glute contraction at the top.
4) Pulse up and down for 3 reps driving heels into the floor and then return all the way down to the floor and repeat for prescribed repetitions.
1) Grab a small band and place it round the thighs just above the knees.
2) Adopt an all fours position on the floor with hands directly under the shoulders and knees directly under the hips.
3) Brace abs and keep hips as stable as possible whilst keeping the leg bent and lifting it up out to the side, squeezing the glutes and leading with the knee.
4) Return to the starting position and then repeat for prescribed repetitions, then do the same on the other leg.
1) Grab a band and tie one end around something stable at around shoulder height.
2) Take hold of the other end of the band with both hands and step slightly back so there is some tension in the band.
3) With palms facing down, leading with the elbows move the band towards your face keeping the elbows high. Squeeze the shoulder blades together.
4) Return back to the start position and repeat for the prescribed repetitions.
1) Grab a small band and place it round your ankles, feet just wider than hip distance.
2) Squat down with hips below knees and chest up.
3) Walk forward in this low position keeping abs braced and chest up.
4) Keep distributing weight through the feet evenly.
5) Repeat for prescribed repetitions.
1) Grab a small band and place one side round one ankle and the other side round the sole of the other foot.
2) Adopt an all fours position on the floor with hands directly under the shoulders and knees under the hips.
3) Brace abs and keep hips as stable as possible.
4) With the leg that has the band around the sole of the foot, flex the foot and push the heel towards the ceiling until you get full glute contraction without moving the hips.
5) Come back down until the knee is nearly back on the floor and repeat for the prescribed repetitions.
6) Complete on the other leg.
1) Grab a small band and place it around both ankles, hip distance apart.
2) Squat down bending the knees and hinging at the hips.
3) Sit into your heels and keep chest lifted and abs braced throughout.
4) Move laterally by pushing against the band and taking small steps.
5) With the other leg resist the band as you step in.
6) Maintain the position and squeeze glutes throughout.
7) Repeat for the prescribed repetitions on one leg and then complete on the other side.
1) Grab a small band and place it around the thighs just above the knees.
2) Set up a bench or a step and barbell.
3) Take a seat on the floor with your legs underneath the bar, your back should be supported by a bench or an elevated step with your shoulder blades being the point of contact.
4) Roll the bar up and over your legs until it is directly above your hips, bend at your knees and walk your feet in towards your glutes.

5) Whilst exhaling, push your knees into the band, squeeze your glutes and drive your heels to lift your hips towards the ceiling. As you do so, keep your chin on your chest keep driving upward until your glutes are fully contracted and your body is parallel with the floor.
6) Inhale and gently relax your glutes to return the barbell back along the same path to the starting position.
7) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
1) Stand with feet hip-width apart and a resistance band looped around both ankles.
2) Engage your core and maintain a slight bend in your knees.
3) Lift one leg out to the side squeezing the glutes, stretching the band, then return to the starting position. Repeat for prescribed repetitions and then repeat on the other side.
1) Sit on the floor with your knees bent, holding a kettlebell with both hands close to your chest.
2) Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the ground, balancing on your sit bones.
3) Twist your torso to one side, lowering the kettlebell towards the floor beside your hip.
4) Return to the centre and twist to the opposite side, alternating sides for each repetition.
5) Focus on controlled movements keeping the chin off the chest and engage your core throughout.
This exercise targets your obliques and improves core strength and rotational stability.
1) Sit on the floor with your knees bent, holding a dumbbell with both hands close to your chest.
2) Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the ground, balancing on your sit bones.
3) Twist your torso to one side, lowering the dumbbell towards the floor beside your hip.
4) Return to the centre and twist to the opposite side, alternating sides for each repetition.
5) Focus on controlled movements keeping the chin off the chest and engage your core throughout. This exercise targets your obliques and improves core strength and rotational stability.
1) Sit on the floor with your knees bent, holding a medicine ball with both hands close to your chest.
2) Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the ground, balancing on your sit bones.
3) Twist your torso to one side, lowering the medicine ball towards the floor beside your hip.
4) Return to the centre and twist to the opposite side, alternating sides for each repetition.
5) Focus on controlled movements keeping the chin off the chest and engage your core throughout.
This exercise targets your obliques and improves core strength and rotational stability.
1) Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a weight plate with both hands, palms facing in.
2) Keep elbows close to your sides, curl the plate towards your shoulders.
3) Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower the plate back down.
4) Repeat for desired reps.
5) Focus on controlled movements and engage your biceps throughout. This exercise targets your biceps and improves arm strength.
1) Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at arm's length, palms facing forward.
2) Keeping your elbows close to your sides, curl the kettlebells towards your shoulders.
3) Pause briefly at the top, then lower the kettlebells back down with control.
4) Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and engaging your biceps throughout.
1) Sit on the ground with your upper back against a bench and a dumbbell across your hips.
2) Hold the dumbbell securely and plant your feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
3) Press through your heels to lift your hips up until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
4) Pause briefly at the top, then lower your hips back down.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
6) Focus on squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.
1) Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
2) Step back with one leg into a reverse lunge, lowering your body until your front thigh is parallel to the ground.
3) Push through your front heel and jump immediately switching legs, performing a reverse lunge on the other side.
4) Continue alternating legs with each repetition.
5) Keep your chest up and core engaged throughout the movement.
This exercise targets your legs, glutes, and core while also improving balance and coordination.
1) Stand with your feet hip-width apart and hands on your hips.
2) Step back with one foot and lower your body until both knees are bent at a 90-degree angle.
3) Pause briefly, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
4) Switch legs and repeat.
5) Focus on keeping your torso upright and engaging your core for stability. This exercise targets your legs and glutes.
1) Start in a seated position with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. Place your hands under shoulders.
2) Lift your hips off the ground, forming a tabletop position with your torso and thighs parallel to the floor.
3) Begin to walk forwards by taking small steps with your hands and feet, moving in one direction.
4) Maintain tension in your core and keep your hips lifted throughout the movement.
5) Continue walking for the desired distance or number of steps.
6) Reverse direction and walk back to your starting point.
7) Focus on controlled movements and keep your core engaged to target your glutes and hip muscles.
1) Stand with your feet hip-width apart and toes pointed slightly outwards.
2) Lower into a squat position, keeping your chest up and knees tracking over your toes.
3) Take small steps forward, staying low to the ground and maintaining the squat position.
4) Continue walking forward for a set distance or number of steps.
5) Focus on keeping your core engaged and your weight distributed evenly on both feet.
6) Reverse the movement to walk backward if desired.
Duck walks target your quads, glutes, and hip flexors while improving lower body strength and mobility.
1) Adopt an all fours position on the floor with hands directly under the shoulders and knees under the hips.
3) Brace abs and keep hips as stable as possible.
4) With one leg, flex the foot and push the heel towards the ceiling until you get full glute contraction without moving the hips.
5) Come back down until the knee is nearly back on the floor and repeat for the prescribed repetitions.
6) Complete on the other leg.
1) Sit on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat. Lean back, resting your upper back against a sturdy surface.
2) Drive through your heels to lift your hips towards the ceiling, squeezing your glutes at the top.
3) Pause briefly, then lower your hips back down.
4) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
5) Focus on squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.
1) Stand tall holding a kettlebell with both hands, held at chest height.
2) Step backward with one leg, lowering your body until both knees are bent at 90-degree angles.
3) Push through the front heel to return to the starting position.
4) Repeat on the opposite leg.
5) Maintain a straight back and engage your core for stability.
This exercise targets your legs and glutes while also improving balance and coordination.
1) Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
2) Take a small step forward with one foot, bending both knees to lower your body.
3) As you lunge, focus on driving your front knee forward over the toes.
4) Keep your torso upright and core engaged.
5) As you stand up think about driving the knee back.
6) Repeat for prescribed amount of reps on one side.
7) Repeat on the other leg.
This exercise targets your quads and improves lower body strength.
Start in a split stance keeping your feet roughly hip-width apart.
Lunge down keeping your weight above your leading leg.
As you stand back up from the lunge, aim to shift your weight back more towards your hips, creating a more vertical shin angle and forward lean.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Hold a dumbbell by your thigh, keeping your other arm free.
Lift one leg off the ground and move the foot behind you whilst leaning forwards slightly.
Keeping your back straight, hinge at the hips to lean further forward, raising your back leg behind you to maintain balance.
Lower until you feel the stretch in your standing leg's hamstring, then return to the starting position.
Place a barbell in a landmine attachment and load the free end of the bar with weight plates.
If your gym doesn't have a landmine attachment you can wedge the end of the barbell in a corner and place a heavy dumbbell over the bar to help keep it in place.
Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, holding the end of the barbell with both hands supinated.
Keeping your arms extended below you and back straight, keep your core and lats braced and stand up from the lengthened position.
In a controller manner, bring the bar back down keeping your posture tight and chest up.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Place a barbell in a landmine attachment and load the free end of the bar with weight plates.
If your gym doesn't have a landmine attachment you can wedge the end of the barbell in a corner and place a heavy dumbbell over the bar to help keep it in place.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the barbell with one hand at shoulder height
Keeping the bar at shoulder height, press the bar up and forwards so that your arm is extended with a slight bend in the elbow at the top of the rep.
In a controller manner, bring the barbell back down to shoulder height to complete the rep.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Place a barbell in a landmine attachment and load the free end of the bar with weight plates.
If your gym doesn't have a landmine attachment you can wedge the end of the barbell in a corner and place a heavy dumbbell over the bar to help keep it in place.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the barbell with both hands at chest height
Keeping the bar at chest height, slowly squat down until your upper and lower legs make a 90-degree angle.
As you exhale, press through your heels to drive up, keeping your quadriceps and glutes engaged throughout the rep.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
From standing, place your feet shoulder width apart with your toes pointing slightly outwards. This will be your starting position
As you inhale, slowly squat down until your upper and lower legs make a 90-degree angle and hold your position.
Hold this position for the recommended amount of time.
When it's time to return to standing, exhale and drive through your heels to stand up.
Place a foam block or two weight plates on the ground, put your heels on the edge of the wedge/plates to create a shoulder width stance.
Once in the correct position, step onto the platform so that your heels are on the edge.
From here, squat down to grab your dumbbells. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, slowly squat down until your upper and lower legs make a 90-degree angle.
As you exhale, press through your heels to drive up, engaging your quadriceps and glutes throughout the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Place a foam block or two weight plates on the ground, put your heels on the edge of the wedge/plates to create a shoulder-width stance.
Once in the correct position, squat down to pick up the dumbbell, resting your palms on the inner surface to keep it sturdy.
Squat up, keeping a neutral posture. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, slowly squat down until your upper and lower legs make a 90-degree angle.
As you exhale, press through your heels to drive up, engaging your quadriceps and glutes in order to do so.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Place a foam block or two weight plates on the ground, put your heels on the edge of the wedge/plates to create a shoulder width stance.
Once in the correct place, step under the bar and unrack the weight so the barbell is rested on your back.
Take a step back so that your heels are on the edge of the wedge/plates. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, slowly squat down until your upper and lower legs make a 90-degree angle.
As you exhale, press through your heels to drive the bar up, engaging your quadriceps and glutes in order to do so.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

For this exercise you will need a heavy rope anchored at its center 15-20 feet away. Standing in front of the rope, take an end one hand with your arm extended at your side. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the movement by rapidly raising one arm to shoulder level as quickly as you can.
As you let that arm drop to the starting position, raise the opposite side.
Continue alternating your left and right arms, whipping the ropes up and down as fast as you can.
From a seated or standing position, lean your head backwards to look up towards to ceiling.
Hold the position for the recommended duration.
From a seated or standing position, interlink your fingers behind your head and gently start to pull your head (and in turn your chin) down towards your chest.
Once your chin is in contact with your chest, hold the position for the recommended amount of time.
From a standing position with your feet hip width appart, lean your head across to your left shoulder.
To increase the stretch, take hold of your head with your left hand and gently pull downward towards your shoulder.
Hold this position for recommended duration and swap sides.
Take hold of a chin up bar with both hands an overhand grip, straight arm and allow the majority of your body weight be 'hung'.
Hold for the recommended duration.
Take a wide stance with your torso upright and your arms by your sides.
Next, keep your legs straight, lean forward and towards your right leg so that your torso is parallel to the floor.
Bring your left arm up so that it is also parallel with the floor.
Hold for recommended duration and swap sides.
Take a standing position with your feet hip width appart.
Next, lean your torso forward and bring your arms straight next to your head so that you are parallel with the floor and reach forward.
Hold for recommended period of time.
Take a standing position with your feet together facing the pole. Hold the pole with both hands at shoulder height.
Then, slowly lean away for the pole.
Hold for the recommended duration.
From a standing position, shoulder width stance with your torso upright.  Keep your left arm by your side and elevate your right arm up.
Gently lean towards your left side, reaching over with your right arm. Maintain a balanced stance with your weight evenly distributed through your stance.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Take a seat on the floor with your right leg extended out in front of you with your left leg bent at a 90 degree angle placed over the top of your right leg.
Place your right elbow on the outside of your left knee with your left hand on the floor behind you.
Bend your right leg so that your right foot comes towards your left glute.
Rotate your torso anti clockwise. Tip: To emphasise this stretch bring your bent leg towards your torso and extend upper rotation.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.

Lay on a mat, on your back with your arms extended out to your sides with your palms facing the floor. Lift your feet off the floor by creating a 90 degree bend at your knees and hips. Tip: Keep your knees together throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
Next, allow your knees to descend towards the right hand side.
Hold for recommeded time.
Next, use your core muscles to bring your lower body back along the same path into the starting position and repeat on other side.
Your starting position will be on your hands and knees in an 'all-fours' position.
Once you are in this position, breathe normally and contract your upper back.
Hold this position for the recommended amount of time.
Take a kneeling positionon a matt, then lower your glutes to your heels and lean forward so that your formarms are flat on the floor.
Hold for the recommended duration.
Lay down on a matt facin up with your arms out to your sides and your legs flat on the floor.
Next, bring both legs towards your chest, place your hands on each knee to stay in position.
Hold for the recommended duration.

From a standing position, bring your right knee up towards your chest and interlink your fingers over your knee to hold the position. To accentuate the stretch using your hands pull your knee closer to your chest.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Take a seat on a mat with your left leg out straight, your right knee bent and relaxed out wide with the sole of your foot gently placed against the inside of your left knee.
Lean your torso towards your left leg take and reach your toes.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.
From a standing position lift your chest towards the ceiling, squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Keeping your hips central and lean back.
Hold for the recommended duration.
Lay down face forward on a matt, place your elbows inline underneath your shoulders.
Next, pushing down with your hands straighten your arms.
Hold for the recommended duration.

Lay down face forward on a matt, place your elbows inline underneath your shoulders and your arms in front of you bent at 90 degrees. Ensure your hips are extended towards the floor and your shoulders are relaxed.
Hold for the recommended duration.
Take a standing position with your feet hip width appart, place the palms of your hands together then push your fingers away from each other with your finder tips.
Hold for recommended duration.
Take a standing position with your feet hip width appart.
Next, take both hands in front so your arms are parallel to the floor with your palms facing down.
Keeping your arm straight, place your right hand over your left hand and gently pull your left hand towards you.
Hold this position for recomended duration and swap sides.
Take a standing position with your feet, hip width appart.
Next, take both hands in front of you and interlink your fingers. Then straighten your arms so the palms of your hands are facing away from you.
Hold this position for recommended duration.
Adopt an 'all fours' position on a mat but have your fingers pointing your knees rather than away from your body. This will be your starting position.
Whilst maintaining a connection to the floor with your palms, gently lean your bodyweight backwards towards your heels until you create the desired stretch in your forearms.
Hold this position for the recommended duration.
Take a wide stance with your torso upright and your arms by your sides.
Next, keep your legs straight, lean forward and towards your right leg. Then reach your right foot with your left hand.
Hold for recommended duration and swap sides.
Take a standing position with your back to the pole. Take your hands behind you to hold the pole.
Then, slowly lean away for the pole.
Hold for the recommended duration.
Lay on a mat, face down, with your legs fully extended and your arms out straight.

Once you are in this position, breathe normally lift your right leg up behind you and cross your left leg keeping your left shoulder on the matt. Bring your right arm towards your torso to support you.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Lay on a mat, face down, with your legs fully extended and your arms out with your hands by your head.
Once you are in this position, breathe normally lift your right leg up behind you and cross your left leg keeping your left shoulder on the matt.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

Stand with a shoulder width stance either next to a wall or doorway. Elevate one of your arms so your elbow is at 90 degrees and your forearm is vertical from the floor. Place your forearm against the upright surface. This is your starting position.
Gently lean your bodyweight forward until you feel the desired stretch come through your lats.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Take a seat on a bench, with both hands flat eitherside of your hips and your fingers pointed behind you.
Next, keep your feet and hands in position and lower your body down from the bench so that your elbow is at 90 degrees.
Hold for recommended duration.
Take a standing position with your feet hip width appart.
Next, take both hand behind you and interlink your fingers. To accentuate the stretch lift your arms away from you.
Hold for recommended duraction.
From a standing or seated position, bring your right arm across your chest.
Next, take your left arm under your right arm and gently pull your left arm in.
Hold for recommened time and swap sides.
Take a standing position, shoulder width stance with your torso upright.
Gently lean forwards from your waist whilst keeping your legs straight. Continue to lean forward until you can feel the stretch come through your hamstrings.
Hold for the recommended duration.
Take a standing position and cross your
Gently lean forwards from your waist whilst keeping your legs straight. Continue to lean forward until you can feel the stretch come through your hamstrings.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap legs.

name_en: Standing Reach Down Hamstring Stretch (Shoulder Width Apart)
instructions_en: Take a wide stance standing position with your torso upright.
Gently lean forwards from your waist whilst keeping your legs straight. Continue to lean forward until you can feel the stretch come through your hamstrings.
Hold for the recommended duration.

name_en: Standing High-Leg Bent Knee Hamstring Stretch
instructions_en: Set up a box/step/bench in front of you at the desired height. Stand upright with your arms beside your torso and adopt a shoulder width stance. This will be your starting position.
Next, take your right foot up and place ontop of the box.
Hold for recommended period and swap sides.
Lay down, face up on a mat with your legs flat on the floor, take the band and loop it over your left foot and gently pull tight.
Keeping your left leg straight pull the band with both hands towards your torso that your leg comes up off the floor.
Make sure the band will not slip off your foot, slowly pull harder on the band so that the stretch becomes greater until you create the desired amount of tension.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Once you've taken a seat in a crossed legged position, extend your right leg straight infront of you.
Next, lean your torso forward and reach your right foot with your right hand.
Hold for recommended period and swap sides.
Take a standing position in front of a bench, your feet should be shoulder width appart.
Next, take your left leg up on the bench so that your foot is flex and your leg is straight. Lean your torso forward and reach your left foot with your left hand.
Hold for recommened duration and swap sides.
Take a seat on a matt with your legs straight infront of you and your torso upright.
Next, lean your torso forward and reach your feet with your hands.
Hold for recommended period.
Take a kneeling position on a matt with your knees apart slightly.
Next, sit back between your feet and slowly lean back with your hands behind you for support. Be sure to keep your knees down.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Lay down, face up on a mat with your legs flat on the floor.
Keeping your left leg straight the and bring your right foot towards the outside of your right hip.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Lay on a mat, face down, with your legs fully extended and flat on the floor.

Next, bring your right foot towards your glute then take a hold of your right foot with your right hand so that your knee bends and your hips are extended towards the floor, stretching your thigh and hip flexors.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

Adopt an upright standing position with your arms by your side. Next, bend your right knee (to a 90 degree angle) so that your ankle is directly behind your knee. Take hold of this ankle with your right hand and gently pull your ankle upward. Tip: To emphasise this stretch, push your knee down towards the ground and push your hips forward.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.
To get into the starting position for this stretch, you need to drop to the bottom of a lunge position. So, take a big step to split your stance and descend so that your back knee meets the floor.
Lean forward so that your bodyweight is being driven onto your front foot and towards the floor through the extension of your pelvis.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Take a standing position, shoulder width stance with your torso upright.
Next, slowly squat down and ensure that your feet are flat on the floor and place your arms around the outside of your knees.
Hold this position for the recommended duration.

name_en: Seated Toe Pull Achilles Stretch (Bent Knee)
instructions_en: Once you have taken a seat on a mat with your legs straight in front of you, bend your knees slightly so that you and can comfortably reach the top of your feet with your hands.
Next, keeping your legs slightly bent gently pull your feet towards your shins.
Hold this position for the recommended duration.
Once you've taken a seat in a crossed legged position, extend your right leg straight infront of you.
Next, lean your torso forward and reach your right foot with your right hand and gently pull your foot towards you.
Hold for recommended period and swap sides.
From a standing position slightly split stance (one foot in front of the other) keeping your front leg straight bend your back leg and lean your torso forward.
Next, lift your front foot towards you so that your heel is still on the floor and you feel the stretch in your calf. For support place your hands on you bent leg.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.

Take a standing position with a slightly split stance (one foot in front of the other) facing a wall. Place you the toes of your front foot on the wall,  keeping your leg straight a slight bend your back leg.  For support place your hands on the wall.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Set up a box/step/bench in front of you at the desired height. Stand upright with your arms beside your torso and adopt a shoulder-width stance. This will be your starting position.
Next, place your right shin ontop of the box so that your knee is bent at 45 degrees, lean your torso forwards and place your hands on the box for support.
Hold for recommended duration and swap sides.

To get into the starting position for this stretch, you need to drop to the bottom of a lunge position. So, take a big step with your right leg to split your stance and descend so that your back knee meets the floor.
Then, bring your left arm straight up to your head and lean to your right.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Take a wide stance and come down into a low squat.
Next, lean your torso forward and take a hold of the inside of your left ankle with your right arm and bring your shoulder to rest on the inside of your right knee.
Then bring your left arm up behind your head and turn your chest towards the ceiling.
Hold for recommended duration and swap sides.
Once you have taken a seat on a mat with your legs flat on the floor.
Keeping your left leg straight the and bring your right foot towards the outside of your right hip.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Lay on your back with your knees bent to a 90 degre angle.
Next, lift your right foot up and place your ankle across your left knee.

With your right arm reach through the gap you have just created with your legs and your left hand around the outside of your left thigh. Then pull your left thigh in towards your chest. Tip: Keep your shoulders and neck relaxed. Straighten your left leg if you wish to emphasise the movement or even stretch your left hamstring at the same time.
Hold for the recommended time and swap sides.
Variation: Foam Roll Glutes.
Once you've taken a seat on a matt with your legs infront of you and a slight bend at the knee.
Next, keeping your leg bent take your right leg over your left leg.
Place your right arm down by your side for stability and place your left arm on your left leg.
Hold for recommended period of time and swap sides.
Once you've taken a seat, place your feet together.

Next, using your left hand bring your right leg towards your chest and take your right arm around your right knee and lock your hands together. To emphasise the stretch bring your bent leg towards your check.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Once you've taken a seat, place your legs straight in front of you.
Next, bring your right leg towards your chest and take your right foot over your left leg. To emphasise the stretch pull your bent leg towards your torso.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Once you've taken a seated, place your feet together.
Next, lean your torso forward and place your hands on the floor in front of you. Ensure your back is straight and your shoulders are relaxed. To accentuate the stretch walk your hands forwards.
Hold for the recommended duration.
Once you've taken a seat in a crossed legged position, lean your torso forward and place your hands flat on the floor. To accentuate the stretch walk your hands and place your forearms on the floor.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Take a standing position parallel to a wall or pole.
Next, place your right hand on the pole and make a slight bend in your knees and place your left ankle on your right knee. To accentuate the stretch increase the bend in your supporting leg.
Hold for recommended duration and swap sides.
Your starting position will be on your hands and knees in an 'all-fours' position.
Once you are in this position, breathe normally and extend your right leg straight behind you and cross your left leg infront of you.
Next, lean your torso foward and plave your forearms flat on the floor.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Set up a box/step/bench in front of you at the desired height. Stand parallel to the box with your arms infront your torso and adopt a shoulder width stance. This will be your starting position.
Next, place your right foot ontop of the box and lean your torso forwards.
Hold for recommended duration and swap sides.
Your starting position will be on your hands and knees (in an 'all-fours' position). Lift your left leg up and off the mat and take hold of your ankle behind your body with your right hand.
At this point you should be feeling a stretch through your quadriceps and hip flexors.
Push your hips towards the floor to emphasise the stretch.
Hold for the recommended time and switch sides.

To get into the starting position for this stretch, you need to drop to the bottom of a lunge position. So, take a big step with your right leg to split your stance and descend so that your back knee meets the floor.
Next, place your left hand on the floor next to you.
Then lean your torso towards your left hand and place your right hand next to your left hand.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Lay down face up with your arms away from your body and your legs flat.
Next, bend your right leg and take it over your left leg so that your right foot is next to your left knee. Then bring your left foot towards your glute.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Once you have taken a seat on the floor, position your legs in a straddle ('V' shape) or as far apart as you can.
Keep your torso facing forward and lean your right shoulder towards your right leg.
Bring your right arm infront of you and your leg arm up over your head.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Once you have taken a seat on the floor, position your legs in a straddle ('V' shape) or as far apart as you can.
Lean your torso towards your right leg and place your hands on the floor either side of your leg about shoulder-width apart. Ensure your back straight and your shoulders are relaxed.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.

To get into the starting position for this stretch, you need to drop to the bottom of a lunge position. So, take a big step with your right leg to split your stance and descend so that your back knee meets the floor.
Lean your torso forward and place your forearms flat on the floor. Be sure to keep your elbows on the right side of your left foot.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

Once you've taken a seat in a crossed legged position, lean your torso forward and place your hands on the floor in front of you. Ensure your back is straight and your shoulders are relaxed. To accentuate the stretch lean more with your torso walk your hand forwards.
Hold for the recommended duration.
Lay on a mat, on your right side, with your right arm down supporting your upper body and your legs fully extended.
Next, bring your left foot towards your hips and place your foot flat on the floor in front of your right quad.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.
Take a standing position next to a pole with your feet together and your right arm holding the pole.
Next, reach your left arm up above your head and take hold of the  pole.
Keeping your body aligned, lean your hips away from the pole
Hold for recommended period of time and swap sides.
Lay down on a mat with the foam roller placed under your lower back/top of your glutes, so that the top of your back it on the map and your hips are elevated.
Next, keep your left leg straight and relaxed, bring your right knee towards your torso and hold your knee with both hands. You should now feel the stretch in your left hip flector.
Hold for recommended duration and swap sides.

name_en: Standing Wide Legged Adductor Stretch (Toes Pointed Forward)
instructions_en: Take a wide stance with your torso upright and your feet forward.
Next, keep your legs straight, lean your torso forward untill your hands come to the floor.
Hold for recommended duration.
Once you have taken a seat on the floor, position your legs in a straddle ('V' shape) or as far apart as you can.
Lean forward with your torso and place your hands on the floor in front of you, about shoulder width apart. Ensure your back is straight and your shoulders are relaxed.
Hold for the recommended duration.
Take a wide stance with your torso upright and your feet forward.
Next, come down to a squat position keeping your torso upright. Place each hand on the inside of your knees and gently pull so that you feel the stretch through the adductors.
Hold for recommended duration.
Once you've taken a seat in an upright position, place your feet together, elbows over your knees and hold your ankles with your hands.
Next, lean your torso forward and apply slight pressure on your knees using your elbows. The increase in pressure will accentuate the stretch.
Hold for recommended duration.
Once you've taken a seat in a crossed legged position, extend your right leg behind your body whilst keeping your knee at a 90-degree angle.
Next, lean your torso forward and place your forearms flat on the floor. Be sure to keep your elbows on the left side of your left foot, this will accentuate the stretch.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.
From a standing position place your right hand at the top of the stick.
Next, take your right arm out to the side of your body and bend you arm to 90 degrees. Turn your wrist slightly so that the stick is behind your arm.
Then using your left arm pull the bottom of stick until you reach your desired stretch.
Hold for recommened time and swap sides.

name_en: Sandbag - Alternating Static Overhead Side Lunges
instructions_en: Stand up straight with the sandbag held above your head. This will be your starting position.

Take an exaggerated step out to one side and plant your foot with your toes slightly pointing outwards. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement so that you minimise the risk of injury. Inhale, push your hips backward and bend the knee of the foot you have stepped out with in order to lower your weight over that thigh. Once your thigh is parallel with the floor, pause for a brief moment, exhale and push through the heel and thigh from that planted foot.
Throughout this process the leg that remained in the starting position should be straight.
Alternate legs as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Leg Press (Wide Stance).

Grab a sandbag with a slightly wider than shoulder width over hand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor until the bag is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the sandbag. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bag gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the sandbag upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the sandbag further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bag and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.
Descend the sandbag back into the starting position by reversing this movement, making sure you keep the bag close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Cleans or Kettlebell Cleans.

Grab the sandbag on each side and lay on a decline bench with the sandbag above your chest. Lift, extending so that it is directly above your chest. Keep your elbows slightly bent. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bag over and beyond your head by bending at your elbows until your upper arms are in line with your torso.
After a brief pause, exhale and bring the bag back into the starting position above your chest.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Pullover, Dumbbell Pullovers, Straight Arm Pulldowns Rope.

name_en: Sandbag - Decline Narrow Grip Chest Press
instructions_en: Grab a sandbag with your hands as close to each other as possible (palms inward) and lay on a decline bench. Have your hands by the side of your chest with a neutral grip. This will be your starting position.
Ensuring that your elbows remain as close to your body whilst remaining parallel to each other throughout the movement, press the bag towards the ceiling in a controlled manner.
Squeeze your triceps at the top of the contraction for a second before reversing the movement back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Narrow Grip Barbell Bench Press.

Grab the sandbag on each side and lay on a flat bench with the sandbag above your chest. Lift, extending so that it is directly above your chest. Keep your elbows slightly bent. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bag over and beyond your head by bending at your elbows until your upper arms are in line with your torso.
After a brief pause, exhale and bring the bag back into the starting position above your chest.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Pullover, Dumbbell Pullovers, Straight Arm Pulldowns Rope.

Grab a sandbag with your hands as close to each other as possible (palms inward) and lay on a flat bench. Have your hands by the side of your chest with a neutral grip. This will be your starting position.
Ensuring that your elbows remain as close to your body whilst remaining parallel to each other throughout the movement, press the bag towards the ceiling in a controlled manner.
Squeeze your triceps at the top of the contraction for a second before reversing the movement back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Narrow Grip Barbell Bench Press.

Grab the sandbag on each side and lay on an incline bench (between 30-45%) with the sandbag above your chest. Lift, extending so that it is directly above your chest. Keep your elbows slightly bent. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bag over and beyond your head by bending at your elbows until your upper arms are in line with your torso.
After a brief pause, exhale and bring the bag back into the starting position above your chest.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Pullover, Dumbbell Pullovers, Straight Arm Pulldowns Rope.
Instructions

Grab a sandbag with your hands as close to each other as possible (palms inward) and lay on an incline bench. Have your hands by the side of your chest with a neutral grip. This will be your starting position.
Ensuring that your elbows remain as close to your body whilst remaining parallel to each other throughout the movement, press the bag towards the ceiling in a controlled manner.
Squeeze your triceps at the top of the contraction for a second before reversing the movement back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Narrow Grip Barbell Bench Press.

name_en: Sandbag - Sit Up To Shoulder Press
instructions_en: Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lay down on your back on the floor bringing the dumbbells beside your chest. Keep your elbows at a 90-degree angle and your forearms perpendicular to the floor. Once here, keep your palms facing away from you, exhale and press the dumbbells towards the ceiling. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale lift your shoulders off the floor towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your torso is vertical to the floor and your chest is very close to your knees.

Without leaning backward or putting your posture into jeopardy, press the dumbbells above your head so that your arms are fully extended, yet slightly flexed/bent at your elbows. Make sure your palms are facing forward at this point
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Cable Crunches.

Grab a sandbag of the required weight and take a seat on the floor. Hold the bag above your hips (flexing slightly at the elbow) and create an imaginary 'V' shape with your body by leaning back and lifting your feet off the floor. Tip: Your knees should be bent and if any pressure comes through your lower back, replace your feet back onto the floor. This is the starting position.
Twist your torso to the right hand side until the bag is just off the floor. Exhale as you twist, making sure that your head follows your shoulders.
Reverse the movement towards the left side keeping your breathing constant and exhaling as the weight is lowered either side.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: V Sit With Plate Rotations or Seated Barbell Twists.

Grab a sandbag and lay flat on your back, your arms should be holding onto the sandbag above your head. Elevate your legs (at full extension) vertically towards the ceiling until you create a 90 degree bend at your hips. Tip: Keep your legs extended throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and start to descend your legs on an arcing motion towards the floor. Continue to descend until your legs are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your legs along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Reverse Crunches.

name_en: Sandbag - Lateral Drag With Press Ups
instructions_en: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and get into a push-up position leaning forward so that your weight is now baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. The sandbag should be placed under your chest to either the right or left side. This will be your starting position.
With your right arm, pull the sandbag from your left-hand side and move it to just outside of your right shoulder.

Whilst inhaling, slowly lower your chest towards the ground whilst flaring your elbows out wide. When you are about an inch or so from the ground, you should have created a triangle with your hand positions and your eye line.

Pause for a brief movement, exhale and press through your palms to return your body along the same path to the starting position. Tip: Squeeze your pectoral muscles as you do so and when you get to the top of the movement, do not lock your elbows out.
Alternate between left and right.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Sandbag lateral drags

To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and get into a push-up position leaning forward so that your weight is now baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. The sandbag should be placed under your chest to either the right or left side. This will be your starting position.
With your right arm, pull the sandbag from your left-hand side and move it to just outside of your right shoulder.
Alternate between left and right.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Sandbag lateral drag with press ups

Grab the sandbag (palms facing each other), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward by bending at your hips; as you bend make sure to keep your back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep your spine in a neutral position. The sandbag should hang directly in front of you as your arms are perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

While keeping the torso stationary, lift the sandbag to your abdomen (as you exhale), elevating elbows towards the ceiling (do not exert any force with the forearm other than holding the sandbag). At the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a second.
Slowly lower the bag to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution:
This exercise is not recommended for people with back issues. A Low Cable Row is a better choice for people with back pain.
Ensure perfect form and never round the back as this can cause injury.
Be cautious with the weights you are using. If you are in doubt, use a lesser weight.
Variations: Low Cable Row or a Bent Over Barbell Row

Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, overhand grip. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders, keep your elbows tucked in close to your ears and have your knuckles facing the ceiling. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement and keep your elbows pinned in close to your ears. This will be your starting position.
Whilst your upper arms remain stationary, inhale and lower the bag down on an arcing motion behind your head until your elbows are at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees).
Pause for a brief moment then exhale and return the bag along the same path to the starting position whilst contracting your triceps.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Skullcrushers or Tricep Dips.
1) Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a sandbag at chest height.
2) Push the sandbag straight out in front of you until your arms are fully extended.
3) Pause briefly, then slowly return the sandbag to the starting position.
4) Keep your core engaged and maintain a stable stance throughout the movement.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
This exercise targets your chest muscles and improves upper body strength.

Grab a sandbag with a shoulder width over hand grip. Stand upright maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor with sandbag just below hips. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Initiate the movement by exhaling and elevating both arms on an arcing motion in front of you. Keep a slight bend at your elbow and continue to elevate upward until your arms are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your arms along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variation: Front Plate Raise or Alternating Front Raises.
Grab the sandbag with both hands, in a standing position, with your arms at full length, palms facing in. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, contract your biceps and bring the sandbag up to shoulder height. Hold the contraction for a second as you squeeze your biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Inhale as you lower the sandbag toward the starting position.
Continue for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Rotating Bicep Curls or Standing Barbell Curls.

Grab a sandbag with a narrow, overhand grip. Adopt a standing position with the bag resting against your thighs, your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back and keep the bag as close to your body as possible throughout the movement.

Exhale and pull the sandbag up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull the sandbag upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the bag.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the sandbag along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Upright Rows or Smith Machine Upright Row

Grab a sandbag with both hands and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the bag with a neutral grip (palms facing together) and stand upright with the bag resting against your thighs. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time lower the sandbag to the floor and rotate your hands so you have an overhand grip (palms facing behind you). You should feel as though you are pushing your hands towards the floor to emphasise the power generated by your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the sandbag above your head until your arms are nearly locked out. As you do so, flip the bag so that your palms are facing forward with the weight running down the back of your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the sandbag along the same path back in front of your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Barbell Clean and Press or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.

Grab a sandbag with both hands and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and your arms remain passive and extended. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. The sandbag should now be just off the floor with your arms being between your legs.
After a brief pause, Push through your heels and thighs to bring your torso back up into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Wide Stance Leg Press or Kettlebell Sumo Squats.

name_en: Sandbag - Sumo Squat To Upright Row
instructions_en: Grab a sandbag with both hands and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and your arms remain passive and extended. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. The sandbag should now be just off the floor with your arms being between your legs.

After a brief pause, Push through your heels and thighs to bring your torso back up. Exhale and pull the sandbag up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull the sandbag upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the bag.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the bag along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Wide Stance Leg Press or Kettlebell Sumo Squats.
Grab a sandbag and place on your upper back taking the weight across your shoulders. Maintain a straight back and a small bend at your knees.
Adopt a shoulder-width stance, maintain a straight back throughout and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to lean forward from your waist. Push your hips and glutes backward as you do so until your torso is just off being parallel to the floor. Your hamstrings should be under tension at this point.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the sandbag to the starting position along the same path, thrusting your hips forward, squeezing your glutes as you do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed. (Remember to keep your back straight throughout the whole movement).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from any back injuries, this may not be the most suitable exercise for you.
Variations: Barbell Good Mornings or Stiff Leg Dumbbell Deadlifts.

Grab a sandbag with both hands and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the sandbag with your palms facing each other and stand upright with the weight in front of you. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the sandbag to your right knee and push your hand towards the floor to emphasise the power generated in your legs.
Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the sandbag to head height with your arm fully extended as you go.
Inhale and return the sandbag along the same path but on the opposite (left) side as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Dumbbell Squat Jumps or TRX Squat Jumps.
Stand up straight with the sandbag placed on your back. This will be your starting position.

Take a step forward (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.

As you exhale, you need to direct force through your quadriceps and glutes in order to drive your body upward and into a jumping motion. As you leave the floor you will reverse your stance by bringing your back leg forward and your front leg back.
As you land, you land into the next repetition by bending at your knees and hips to create right angles at the joints of your front leg, inhale as you do so.
Alternate in this manner and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Walking Lunges or Barbell Lunges.
Stand up straight with the sandbag placed on your chest. This will be your starting position.

Take a step forward (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.

As you exhale, you need to direct force through your quadriceps and glutes in order to drive your body upward and into a jumping motion. As you leave the floor you will reverse your stance by bringing your back leg forward and your front leg back.
As you land, you land into the next repetition by bending at your knees and hips to create right angles at the joints of your front leg, inhale as you do so.
Alternate in this manner and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Walking Lunges or Barbell Lunges.
Whilst in a standing position, grab the sandbag and place across your back. This will be your starting position.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bag to the starting position along the same path, aggressively push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads and glutes to do so. Driving your hips forward is key in this movement as that will help to give you lift from the ground. Just before you reach a standing position, jump as high as you can.
Land softly at your knees by going straight into the next repetition. If you are at the end of your set, remember to allow a bend at the knees to soften the landing.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squat Jumps or Kettlebell Squat Jumps.
Stand upright holding the sandbag shoulder width apart allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Inhale and descend into a press up position. Place the bag on the ground and jump backward with your feet so that your legs are fully extended and your body is supported by your toes and hands.

Exhale and aggressively press through your hands by extending your elbow joints and create enough upward motion to be able to jump your legs back underneath your body so that when you lift the sandbag off the floor, you should be in the bottom of a squat.
Drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the sandbag. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bag gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the sandbag upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the bag further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bag and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to press the sandbag above your head until your elbows are locked out. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Stand up straight with your feet shoulder width apart holding the sandbag on each side, maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Your first movement will be squatting as you rotate your torso to the right and bring the sandbag diagonally across your body until it is below the hip and next to the right knee.

Exhale and rotate your core so that your hands move up and across your body (in a straight diagonal line). Bringing your arms above your left shoulder. Keep your back and arms straight as you do so. Try to minimise the rotation of your hips so that your core is taking the load.
Once you have turned as far as your torso will allow, pause for a brief moment before inhaling and returning your arms back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Reverse Cable Woodchops.

Stand up straight with your feet shoulder width apart holding the sandbag on each side, maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Your first movement will be squatting as you rotate your torso to the left and bring the sandbag diagonally across your body until it is below the hip and next to the left knee.

Exhale and rotate your core so that your hands move up and across your body (in a straight diagonal line). Bringing your arms above your right shoulder. Keep your back and arms straight as you do so. Try to minimise the rotation of your hips so that your core is taking the load.
Once you have turned as far as your torso will allow, pause for a brief moment before inhaling and returning your arms back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Reverse Cable Woodchops.

Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, holding the bag itself and not the handles. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the sandbag by bending at your elbows until the bag is over your right shoulder.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bag back into the starting position and over to your left shoulder by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Those with back problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variations: Sandbag Shoulder Press or Smith Machine Shoulder Press.
Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width stance with your feet resting the sandbag on your right shoulder holding it in the middle with both hands. This will be your starting position.
Bend down slightly then as you spring up extending through your knees and ankle joints propel the sandbag upward off of your right shoulder over to the left and slowly lowering.
Maintain a straight back throughout this movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Grab a sandbag with a shoulder width over hand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor until the bag is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the sandbag. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bag gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the sandbag upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the bag further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bag and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to press the sandbag above your head until your elbows are locked out. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Descend the sandbag back into the starting position by reversing this movement, making sure to keep the bag close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Clean & Jerk or Barbell Cleans.

Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, overhand grip. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the sandbag by bending at your elbows until the bag is below your chin.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bag back into the starting position by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Those with back problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variations: Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Smith Machine Shoulder Press.
Stand up straight with the sandbag held above your head. This will be your starting position.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the sandbag to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

Stand up straight and place the sandbag so that it is resting on top of your forearms. This will be your starting position.
Position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight back.

Begin to lower the sandbag by bending the knees as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the floor). Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front. If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the sandbag as you exhale by pushing the floor with the ball of your foot mainly as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Caution: This exercise is not to be taken lightly as you are applying a lot of pressure to your arms when lifting the barbell. It is always a good idea to have a spotter as they can help you keep proper form. Also, make sure that your back is kept straight as bending it can cause an injury.
Stand up straight with the sandbag placed across your chest. This will be your starting position.
Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return to the starting position where you will then extend the sandbag above your head and hold.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.
Stand Up straight with the sandbag held above your head. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Pick one foot up off the floor and cross it behind your other leg. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heel as you sit back into the movement until your thigh is either parallel or just past to the floor.
Drive up through your standing leg.
Alternate legs as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Reverse Lunges or Sitting Squats.

Take an exaggerated step out to one side and plant your foot with your toes slightly pointing outwards. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement so that you minimise the risk of injury. Inhale, push your hips backward and bend the knee of the foot you have stepped out with in order to lower your weight over that thigh. Once your thigh is parallel with the floor, pause for a brief moment, exhale and push through the heel and thigh from that planted foot.
Throughout this process the leg that remained in the starting position should be straight.
Alternate legs as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Leg Press (Wide Stance).

4) Pause briefly in the lunge position, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
5) Repeat for the desired reps and then switch legs.
6) Focus on maintaining good posture and engaging your leg muscles throughout the movement.

1) Grab hold of a bar with an overhand grip, and position yourself underneath with your feet elevated on a bench or box.
2) Have hands just wider than shoulder width apart.
3) Keep your body straight from head to heels, engage your core, and pull your chest towards the bar drawing the elbows down.
4) Lower yourself back down with control.
